subject: Project Management
title: Kanban in Education
description: A quick, practical guide to Kanban in Education.

hook.text: Teachers in Sweden gave students Kanban boards to manage their own assignments.

buildup.text: Students moved tasks from 'To Do' to 'Working On' to 'Done' — seeing their own progress.

discovery.text: Visual progress boosted motivation. Students felt ownership over their learning.

twist.text: Younger students took to Kanban faster than adults — they had no bad habits to unlearn.

climax.text: If a 10-year-old can manage work with Kanban, the system's simplicity is its genius.

punchline.text: Simple enough for a child. Powerful enough for Toyota.

quiz.question: What happened when students used Kanban boards?
options: Motivation improved because students could see their progress
